name: Collsesplanes and Sant Sadurní d'Osormort Loop
name_original: Desde Espinelves a Turó de Can Selva – circular con vistas al Montseny
description: This moderate 19.0-mile road cycling route from Espinelves offers scenic views of the Montseny massif and takes about 1 hour and 42 minutes.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Espinelves are situated within the Montseny and Guilleries massifs, offering a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region features extensive forests of oaks, firs, and chestnut trees, providing shaded routes and varied terrain. Cyclists encounter winding country roads with constant changes in vegetation and elevation, ranging from gentle slopes to challenging climbs. This area is characterized by its natural beauty and a network of quiet, secondary roads connecting rural villages. Best road cycling routes around Espinelves The most popular road cycling route is Collsesplanes and Sant Sadurní d'Osormort Loop, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 43 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and rolling sections. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coll Formic to Viladrau – return through the Montseny Natural Park, a difficult 27.2 miles (43.7 km) path. This route includes significant elevation changes through the Montseny Natural Park. Local road cyclists also love the Osor Road – Els Nínxols of Osor loop from Viladrau, a 58.3 miles (93.8 km) trail leading through forested areas, often completed in about 4 hours 55 minutes. Road cycling around Espinelves is defined by the lush forests of Montseny and Guilleries, varied terrain, and winding country roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Espinelves are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 2,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Espinelves's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Espinelves?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Espinelves, offering a diverse range of experiences. You'll find a good mix of moderate and difficult routes, with 11 moderate and 12 difficult options to choose from.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Espinelves?
A: The terrain around Espinelves is quite varied, characterized by the lush forests of the Montseny and Guilleries massifs. You'll encounter winding country roads with constant changes in vegetation and elevation, ranging from gentle slopes to challenging climbs. The region is known for its quiet, secondary roads connecting rural villages, providing a scenic and engaging ride.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: Yes, Espinelves is situated in an area with significant elevation changes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995637581/coll-formic-to-viladrau-return-through-the-montseny-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Formic to Viladrau – return through the Montseny Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 43.7 km path, feature substantial climbs through the Montseny Natural Park. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33990143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osor Road – Els Nínxols of Osor loop from Viladrau&lt;/a&gt;, which is 93.8 km long and includes over 1700 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You'll cycle through extensive forests of oaks, firs, and chestnut trees. Keep an eye out for the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/226426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matagalls (1697 m)&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1687529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home (Man's Hill)&lt;/a&gt;, two prominent peaks in the area. Some routes may also pass by charming villages and the historic Romanesque church of Sant Vicenç d'Espinelves.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995638792/collsesplanes-and-sant-sadurni-d-osormort-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collsesplanes and Sant Sadurní d'Osormort Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 30.6 km circular route with varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18231610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll del Buc (728 m) – Viladrau loop from Viladrau&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 57.8 km loop.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Espinelves?
A: The region's extensive forests provide shaded routes, which can be particularly pleasant during warmer months. However, the varied terrain and natural beauty make it appealing across seasons. Consider spring and autumn for milder temperatures and vibrant foliage, while summer offers shaded relief. Winter cycling is also possible, but be prepared for cooler, potentially humid conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Espinelves?
A: The road cycling routes around Espinelves are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet, winding country roads, the diverse landscapes, and the immersive experience of riding through the lush forests of Montseny and Guilleries.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls or natural pools?
A: While specific road cycling routes might not directly lead to them, the wider area around Espinelves is home to several beautiful water features. You can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4503632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font d'en Prat&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3272326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pools of the Osor stream&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular spots for nature exploration and can be accessed from nearby points along some cycling routes.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While many routes in the area feature significant elevation changes, there are moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995638792/collsesplanes-and-sant-sadurni-d-osormort-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collsesplanes and Sant Sadurní d'Osormort Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with varied terrain and rolling sections, making it a good choice for those looking for a less strenuous ride compared to the more difficult climbs.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Montseny Natural Park?
A: Absolutely. The region is nestled within the Montseny massif, and many routes offer stunning views of the natural park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995637581/coll-formic-to-viladrau-return-through-the-montseny-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Formic to Viladrau – return through the Montseny Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, takes you directly through the park, providing breathtaking vistas and significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any longer road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33990143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osor Road – Els Nínxols of Osor loop from Viladrau&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option. This difficult route spans 93.8 km and leads through extensive forested areas, typically taking nearly 5 hours to complete with considerable climbing.
tips: Short but hard ramp, good for Zone 3 efforts.

It is believed that this Romanesque church was built in 937, being rebuilt in the 11th century.

After some earthquakes in the area, the bell tower was rebuilt in the XV.
The Pont de Bojons is a Romanesque work of Sant Sadurní d'Osormort protected as a cultural asset of local interest.
Very nice port to shoot. With plenty of shade, quiet in terms of traffic and very sustained and constant. Nice views of the Guillerias and Rupit from the top.
Great views, stop and catch your breath.
Nice steady climb out of Arbucies with some great views over Montseny natural park. Petrol station at the top if you need food or drink.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the exact trailhead in Espinelves are not provided, Espinelves is the designated starting point for this circular route. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village, often near public spaces or local amenities, suitable for starting your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montseny region, including the area around Espinelves, offers beautiful cycling conditions during spring, summer, and autumn. These seasons generally provide pleasant weather for enjoying the scenic views and well-surfaced roads. Winter cycling is possible but may require checking local weather conditions for potential cold or wet spells.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in the Guilleries Massif?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling on public roads within the Guilleries Massif or the Montseny area. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning natural parks, and ensure you stay on designated routes.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While the roads are generally well-paved, it involves approximately 671 meters of elevation gain over 30.5 kilometers. Moderate routes in this region typically require good fitness and can involve significant climbing. Beginners with some experience and good physical condition might manage it, but it's not ideal for absolute novices.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions and terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The roads in the Montseny area are often described as very well surfaced, particularly on classic loops. Given its 'moderate' difficulty, you can expect predominantly paved and rideable surfaces. While some segments in the broader Montseny area can be unpaved on more challenging routes, this specific route should offer mostly good road conditions suitable for a racebike.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: As the name suggests, this route offers circular with views of Montseny. You can expect stunning scenery of the mountainous landscapes of the Montseny massif. The region is known for its beautiful and solitary roads, providing impressive landscape views, especially during descents.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting points. You'll encounter mountain passes such as Coll del Buc (728 m), Collsesplanes Pass, and Revell Pass. You'll also pass the Church of Sant Sadurní d'Osormort, the Pont de Bojons, and the Collsesplanes Summit.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. Notably, about 50% of the route follows the Via cicloturística InterCatalunya. Smaller portions also align with [CIMA GI02] Revell - Els Cortals (16%) and TransCatalunya. Catalunya Central - Pirineus (12%), among others.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and generally, road cycling is not considered dog-friendly in the same way hiking trails might be. Dogs are typically not able to keep pace safely with a road bike for 30 kilometers, especially with significant elevation changes. It's best to leave your canine companions at home for this particular adventure.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the route?
faqs.answer: Espinelves, as the starting and ending point, is a small village that may offer limited local amenities like cafes or small restaurants. For more options, you might need to explore larger towns in the Montseny region. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, as services might be sparse along the route itself.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 30.5-kilometer route is approximately 1 hour and 42 minutes of active cycling time. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the views or take breaks.
